title: sida dubia
intro: Sida dubia, also known as Indian jute, is a plant species that belongs to the Malvaceae family and is native to tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. It is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Sida dubia is often prepared as an infusion or decoction in herbal teas, and its dried roots and leaves are also used in traditional remedies.
